description: Package name patterns and string indicators associated with DPRK Contagious Interview / PurpleBravo / Tenacious Pungsan campaigns. Used as a name-pattern feed by the dependency-resolution pipeline and as a content-signature feed by the source scanner.

name_patterns.description: DPRK frequently ships fake vite plugins/loaders with logger-style names. The pattern alone is not malicious — pair with low-age + suspicious owner + install-time exec to escalate.
name_patterns.source: Socket Threat Research, Datadog Tenacious Pungsan, Unit 42

name_patterns.description: Recurring DPRK pattern: SDK / logger / debug shims as cover for credential-stealing payload (pino-debugger, debug-fmt, debug-glitz, logkitx, logger-base, dev-log-core).
Specific package-name shapes flagged in Socket / Phylum coverage of DPRK 2025-2026 waves.
name_patterns.source: Socket, Phylum, Hacker News reporting on 1700-package DPRK wave

content_signatures.description: HexEval loader: long hex blob decoded into a buffer at module load. Followed by eval() or new Function(). Hallmark of DPRK loaders since Sep 2025.

Public family names attached to DPRK Contagious Interview malware. Their literal appearance in source is almost always a researcher comment, but it is also a clear high-signal indicator that the file is part of a tracked sample.
content_signatures.source: Mandiant, Unit 42, Datadog, Microsoft Threat Intel

content_signatures.description: Recurring victim-IP enumeration endpoints used by DPRK loaders to fingerprint targets before stage-2 delivery.
content_signatures.source: Silent Push, Recorded Future PurpleBravo report

lure_indicators.description: README / repo-description language that is characteristic of recruiter-lure repos. Combine with low age + single-contributor + recent npm deps to escalate.
Lure repos sometimes ask the candidate to disable Docker/AV. Strong red flag.
Recruiter-lure repos commonly redirect candidates to Google Docs for instructions. Not malicious alone — only meaningful in combination with other lure indicators.
